item.title: [Daily] Skills.sh snapshot (2026-02-27)
item.description: Daily snapshot. Providers: skills.sh All Time skills: 40546 Trending skills: 21063 Hot skills: 3783 Top All Time: find-skills (346846) Top Trending: agent-tools (16104) Top Hot: azure-compute (489)

item.title: [All Time] New entry (#46): nano-banana-2
item.description: New entry in All Time leaderboard. Rank: #46 Installs: 19712 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Generate images with Google Gemini 3.1 Flash Image Preview (Nano Banana 2) via inference.sh CLI. Capabilities: text-to-image, image editing, multi-image input (up to 14 images), Google Search grounding. Triggers: nano banana 2, nanobanana 2, gemini 3.1 flash image, gemini 3 1 flash image preview, google image generation

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#2): frontend-design
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #2 Installs: 194 Source: anthropics/skills Description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#21): talking-head-production
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #21 Installs: 69 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Talking head video production with AI avatars, lipsync, and voiceover. Covers portrait requirements, audio quality, OmniHuman, PixVerse lipsync, Dia TTS. Use for: spokesperson videos, course content, social media, presentations, demos. Triggers: talking head, avatar video, lipsync, lip sync, ai spokesperson, virtual presenter, ai presenter, omnihuman, talking avatar, video presenter, ai talking head, presenter video, ai face video

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#22): optimize-for-ai
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #22 Installs: 17 Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Optimize content for AI search engines — ChatGPT, Perplexity, Claude, Gemini, Google AI Overviews. Use when the user asks about AI SEO, AISO, getting cited by AI, appearing in AI answers, answer engine optimization, AEO, GEO, LLMO, AI Overviews, zero-click search, or how to appear in ChatGPT/Perplexity results. For traditional SEO, see diagnose-seo.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#23): audit
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #23 Installs: 17 Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Run a comprehensive SEO audit on a website covering technical health, on-page optimization, content quality, and backlink profile. Use when the user asks for an SEO audit, site review, SEO health check, "what's wrong with my SEO", website analysis, or a full diagnostic of their site's search performance. For speed-specific issues, see audit-speed. For technical crawl/index issues only, see diagnose-seo.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#24): fix-linking
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #24 Installs: 17 Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Design or audit internal link structure for a website. Use when the user asks about internal linking, link architecture, orphan pages, PageRank flow, anchor text, site structure, silo architecture, or why pages aren't ranking despite good content. For external link building, see build-links.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#25): migrate-site
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #25 Installs: 17 Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Guide a website migration without losing rankings — domain moves, CMS switches, URL restructures, HTTP to HTTPS, or redesigns. Use when the user asks about site migration, domain change, CMS migration, URL restructure, redesign SEO impact, redirect mapping, or how to move a site safely.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#26): build-clusters
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #26 Installs: 17 Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Build a topical authority cluster from a seed keyword or topic. Use when the user asks about topic clusters, content silos, pillar pages, topical authority, content architecture, or how to structure content around a theme. For finding the right keywords, see find-keywords. For writing individual pieces, see brief.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#27): audit-speed
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #27 Installs: 17 Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Deep Core Web Vitals and page speed audit. Use when the user asks about page speed, Core Web Vitals, LCP, CLS, INP, FCP, TTFB, Lighthouse scores, why a page is slow, performance optimization, or resource size analysis. For broader technical SEO issues, see diagnose-seo.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#28): target-serp
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #28 Installs: 17 Source: calm-north/seojuice-skills Description: Identify and capture SERP features for target keywords — featured snippets, People Also Ask boxes, knowledge panels, and rich results. Use when the user asks about featured snippets, position zero, PAA optimization, rich results, schema markup for SERP features, or how to get more SERP real estate.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#29): character-design-sheet
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #29 Installs: 68 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Character consistency across AI-generated images with reference sheets and LoRA techniques. Covers turnaround views, expression sheets, color palettes, and style consistency tricks. Use for: character design, game art, illustration, animation, comics, visual novels. Triggers: character design, character sheet, character consistency, character reference, turnaround sheet, expression sheet, character art, consistent character, character concept, reference sheet, character creation, oc design, character bible

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#30): prd
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #30 Installs: 19 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Generate high-quality Product Requirements Documents (PRDs) for software systems and AI-powered features. Includes executive summaries, user stories, technical specifications, and risk analysis.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#31): documentation-writer
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #31 Installs: 17 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Diátaxis Documentation Expert. An expert technical writer specializing in creating high-quality software documentation, guided by the principles and structure of the Diátaxis technical documentation authoring framework.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#32): git-commit
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #32 Installs: 21 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Execute git commit with conventional commit message analysis, intelligent staging, and message generation. Use when user asks to commit changes, create a git commit, or mentions "/commit". Supports: (1) Auto-detecting type and scope from changes, (2) Generating conventional commit messages from diff, (3) Interactive commit with optional type/scope/description overrides, (4) Intelligent file staging for logical grouping

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#33): java-springboot
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #33 Installs: 17 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Get best practices for developing applications with Spring Boot.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#34): ef-core
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #34 Installs: 16 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Get best practices for Entity Framework Core

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#35): python-mcp-server-generator
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #35 Installs: 16 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Generate a complete MCP server project in Python with tools, resources, and proper configuration

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#36): conventional-commit
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #36 Installs: 16 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Prompt and workflow for generating conventional commit messages using a structured XML format. Guides users to create standardized, descriptive commit messages in line with the Conventional Commits specification, including instructions, examples, and validation.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#37): dotnet-best-practices
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #37 Installs: 16 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Ensure .NET/C# code meets best practices for the solution/project.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#38): azure-devops-cli
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #38 Installs: 16 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Manage Azure DevOps resources via CLI including projects, repos, pipelines, builds, pull requests, work items, artifacts, and service endpoints. Use when working with Azure DevOps, az commands, devops automation, CI/CD, or when user mentions Azure DevOps CLI.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#39): refactor
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #39 Installs: 16 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Surgical code refactoring to improve maintainability without changing behavior. Covers extracting functions, renaming variables, breaking down god functions, improving type safety, eliminating code smells, and applying design patterns. Less drastic than repo-rebuilder; use for gradual improvements.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#40): power-apps-code-app-scaffold
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #40 Installs: 16 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Scaffold a complete Power Apps Code App project with PAC CLI setup, SDK integration, and connector configuration

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#41): aspire
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #41 Installs: 16 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Aspire skill covering the Aspire CLI, AppHost orchestration, service discovery, integrations, MCP server, VS Code extension, Dev Containers, GitHub Codespaces, templates, dashboard, and deployment. Use when the user asks to create, run, debug, configure, deploy, or troubleshoot an Aspire distributed application.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#42): remember-interactive-programming
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #42 Installs: 16 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: A micro-prompt that reminds the agent that it is an interactive programmer. Works great in Clojure when Copilot has access to the REPL (probably via Backseat Driver). Will work with any system that has a live REPL that the agent can use. Adapt the prompt with any specific reminders in your workflow and/or workspace.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#43): vscode-ext-commands
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #43 Installs: 16 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Guidelines for contributing commands in VS Code extensions. Indicates naming convention, visibility, localization and other relevant attributes, following VS Code extension development guidelines, libraries and good practices

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#44): next-intl-add-language
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #44 Installs: 16 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Add new language to a Next.js + next-intl application

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#46): data-analysis
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #46 Installs: 15 Source: supercent-io/skills-template Description: Analyze datasets to extract insights, identify patterns, and generate reports. Use when exploring data, creating visualizations, or performing statistical analysis. Handles CSV, JSON, SQL queries, and Python pandas operations.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#47): writing-plans
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #47 Installs: 32 Source: obra/superpowers Description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#48): audit-website
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #48 Installs: 30 Source: squirrelscan/skills Description: Audit websites for SEO, performance, security, technical, content, and 15 other issue cateories with 150+ rules using the squirrelscan CLI. Returns LLM-optimized reports with health scores, broken links, meta tag analysis, and actionable recommendations. Use to discover and asses website or webapp issues and health.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#49): boost-prompt
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #49 Installs: 15 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Interactive prompt refinement workflow: interrogates scope, deliverables, constraints; copies final markdown to clipboard; never writes code. Requires the Joyride extension.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#50): devops-rollout-plan
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #50 Installs: 15 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Generate comprehensive rollout plans with preflight checks, step-by-step deployment, verification signals, rollback procedures, and communication plans for infrastructure and application changes

item.title: [Trending] New entry (#49): related-skill
item.description: New entry in Trending leaderboard. Rank: #49 Installs: 1174 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Discover and install related skills from inference.sh skill registry. Helps find complementary skills for your AI workflow. Use for: skill discovery, workflow expansion, capability exploration. Triggers: related skills, find skills, skill discovery, complementary skills, expand workflow, more capabilities, similar skills, skill suggestions

item.title: [Trending] New entry (#50): ai-product-photography
item.description: New entry in Trending leaderboard. Rank: #50 Installs: 1171 Source: inference-sh-9/skills Description: Generate professional AI product photography and commercial images. Models: FLUX, Imagen 3, Grok, Seedream for product shots, lifestyle images, mockups. Capabilities: studio lighting, lifestyle scenes, packaging, e-commerce photos. Use for: e-commerce, Amazon listings, Shopify, marketing, advertising, mockups. Triggers: product photography, product shot, commercial photography, e-commerce images, amazon product photo, shopify images, product mockup, studio product shot, lifestyle product image, advertising photo, packshot, product render, product image ai

item.title: [Hot] Rank down (15 → 18): parallel-task
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #15 Current rank: #18 Delta: -3 Installs: 29 (prev 18) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Execute plan files by launching multiple parallel subagents to complete tasks simultaneously. Triggers on explicit "/parallel-task" commands.

item.title: [Hot] Rank down (3 → 16): read-github
item.description:
Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #3 Current rank: #16 Delta: -13 Installs: 29 (prev 19) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Read and search GitHub repository documentation via gitmcp.io MCP service.
**WHEN TO USE:** - User provides a GitHub URL - User mentions a specific repo in owner/repo format - User asks "what does this repo do?", "read the docs for X repo", or similar - User wants to search code or docs within a repo

item.title: [Hot] Rank down (4 → 7): openai-docs-skill
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #4 Current rank: #7 Delta: -3 Installs: 29 (prev 19) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Query the OpenAI developer documentation via the OpenAI Docs MCP server using CLI (curl/jq). Use whenever a task involves the OpenAI API (Responses, Chat Completions, Realtime, etc.), OpenAI SDKs, ChatGPT Apps SDK, Codex, MCP integrations, endpoint schemas, parameters, limits, or migrations and you need up-to-date official guidance.

item.title: [Hot] Rank down (5 → 20): vet
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #5 Current rank: #20 Delta: -15 Installs: 28 (prev 19) Source: imbue-ai/vet Description: Run vet immediately after ANY logical unit of code changes. Do not batch your changes, do not wait to be asked to run vet, make sure you are proactive.

item.title: [Hot] Rank down (9 → 17): swarm-planner
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #9 Current rank: #17 Delta: -8 Installs: 29 (prev 18) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: [EXPLICIT INVOCATION ONLY] Creates dependency-aware implementation plans optimized for parallel multi-agent execution.

item.title: [Hot] Rank up (10 → 8): plan-harder
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #10 Current rank: #8 Delta: +2 Installs: 29 (prev 18) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Use when user specfically says 'plan harder'.

item.title: [Hot] Rank up (12 → 4): llm-council
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #12 Current rank: #4 Delta: +8 Installs: 30 (prev 18) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Orchestrate a configurable, multi-member CLI planning council (Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, OpenCode, or custom) to produce independent implementation plans, anonymize and randomize them, then judge and merge into one final plan. Use when you need a robust, bias-resistant planning workflow, structured JSON outputs, retries, and failure handling across multiple CLI agents.

item.title: [Hot] Rank up (13 → 9): agent browser
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #13 Current rank: #9 Delta: +4 Installs: 29 (prev 18) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: A fast Rust-based headless browser automation CLI with Node.js fallback that enables AI agents to navigate, click, type, and snapshot pages via structured commands.

item.title: [Hot] Rank up (14 → 10): super-swarm-spark
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #14 Current rank: #10 Delta: +4 Installs: 29 (prev 18) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Only to be triggered by explicit super-swarm-spark commands.

item.title: [Hot] Rank up (16 → 11): parallel-task-spark
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #16 Current rank: #11 Delta: +5 Installs: 29 (prev 18) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Only to be triggered by explicit /parallel-task-spark commands.

item.title: [Hot] Rank up (17 → 12): role-creator
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #17 Current rank: #12 Delta: +5 Installs: 29 (prev 18) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Create and install Codex custom agent roles in ~/.codex/config.toml, generate role config files, enforce supported keys, and guide users through required role inputs (model, reasoning effort, developer_instructions).

item.title: [Hot] Rank up (18 → 13): codex-subagent
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #18 Current rank: #13 Delta: +5 Installs: 29 (prev 18) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Spawn Codex subagents via background shell to offload context-heavy work. Use for: deep research (3+ searches), codebase exploration (8+ files), multi-step workflows, exploratory tasks, long-running operations, documentation generation, or any other task where the intermediate steps will use large numbers of tokens.

item.title: [Hot] Rank up (2 → 1): azure-compute
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #2 Current rank: #1 Delta: +1 Installs: 489 (prev 592) Source: microsoft/github-copilot-for-azure Description: Recommend Azure VM sizes, VM Scale Sets (VMSS), and configurations based on workload requirements, performance needs, and budget constraints. No Azure account required — uses public documentation and the Azure Retail Prices API. USE FOR: recommend VM size, which VM should I use, choose Azure VM, VM for web/database/ML/batch/HPC, GPU VM, compare VM sizes, cheapest VM, best VM for workload, VM pricing, cost estimate, burstable/compute/memory/storage optimized VM, confidential computing, VM trade-offs, VM families, VMSS, scale set recommendation, autoscale VMs, load balanced VMs, VMSS vs VM, scale out, horizontal scaling, flexible orchestration. DO NOT USE FOR: deploying VMs or VMSS, deploying apps (use azure-deploy), looking up existing VMs (use azure-resource-lookup), cost optimization of running VMs (use azure-cost-optimization), non-VM services like App Service or AKS.

item.title: [Hot] Rank up (20 → 14): frontend-design
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #20 Current rank: #14 Delta: +6 Installs: 29 (prev 18) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.

item.title: [Hot] Rank up (21 → 15): planner
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #21 Current rank: #15 Delta: +6 Installs: 29 (prev 18) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Create comprehensive, phased implementation plans with sprints and atomic tasks. Use when user says: "make a plan", "create a plan", "plan this out", "plan the implementation", "help me plan", "design a plan", "draft a plan", "write a plan", "outline the steps", "break this down into tasks", "what's the plan for", or any similar planning request. Also triggers on explicit "/planner" or "/plan" commands.

item.title: [Hot] Rank up (6 → 5): context7
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #6 Current rank: #5 Delta: +1 Installs: 29 (prev 18) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Fetch up-to-date library documentation via Context7 API. Use PROACTIVELY when: (1) Working with ANY external library (React, Next.js, Supabase, etc.) (2) User asks about library APIs, patterns, or best practices (3) Implementing features that rely on third-party packages (4) Debugging library-specific issues (5) Need current documentation beyond training data cutoff (6) AND MOST IMPORTANTLY, when you are installing dependencies, libraries, or frameworks you should ALWAYS check the docs to see what the latest versions are. Do not rely on outdated knowledge. Always prefer this over guessing library APIs or using outdated knowledge.

item.title: [Hot] Rank up (7 → 6): gemini-computer-use
item.description: Hot leaderboard rank change. Previous rank: #7 Current rank: #6 Delta: +1 Installs: 29 (prev 18) Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Build and run Gemini 2.5 Computer Use browser-control agents with Playwright. Use when a user wants to automate web browser tasks via the Gemini Computer Use model, needs an agent loop (screenshot → function_call → action → function_response), or asks to integrate safety confirmation for risky UI actions.
